when_used_explanation: Call has not been disposed yet, therefore, no disposition has been provided
Used when a subject is arrested specifically for a violation of AB109 post-release community supervision conditions.
Used when an incident involves an AB109 supervisee but does not result in an arrest.
Used when a report is taken documenting AB109-related activity or compliance without immediate enforcement action.
Used when a CHP or fire incident is still active.
Used for internal or administrative actions such as documentation, coordination, or status updates that are not enforcement-related.
Used when a subject is taken into custody based on probable cause for a criminal offense.
Used when a call for service is canceled before deputies arrive or before any action is taken.
Used when a subject in mental health crisis is taken into custody under crisis intervention protocols.
Used when deputies respond to a mental health crisis and document the incident without making an arrest.
Used when a subject is cited and released in lieu of arrest for a misdemeanor or infraction.
Used when the matter is civil in nature and law enforcement action is not appropriate.
Used when a CHP or fire incident has closed.
Used when a specialized crisis response or mental health team is deployed or involved.
Used when an alarm or reported incident is determined to be false or unfounded upon arrival.
Used when deputies conduct a consensual field contact and document it without enforcement action.
Used when deputies arrive but the involved parties or issue are no longer present.
Used when deputies resolve the situation through appropriate action that does not require a report or arrest.
Used when documentation is required but no investigation or enforcement is conducted.
Used when a reporting party declines to have a report taken after deputy contact.
Used when deputies assist another law enforcement or public safety agency.
Used when deputies provide assistance or help that is non-enforcement in nature.
Used when a report will be completed later due to time constraints or priority conflicts.
Used when a requested service is completed, such as standby or welfare assistance.
Used when deputies add information to an existing report or case.
Used when an investigation determines the reported incident did not occur.
Used when deputies are unable to locate the subject, victim, or involved party.
Used when a subject is arrested based on an outstanding arrest warrant.
